summary: Phase 6 end-to-end (backend + frontend) tests. 43/44 backend tests PASS (1 skipped by design). Frontend smoke covers /firms (41 cards), /articleship (progress+forms), /community (9 categories), /study-groups (5+ groups), /firm-match, and dashboard Phase-6 bento — ALL testids present and pages render with editorial dark aesthetic. Backend: firms list+detail+tier/city/sort/search filters, firm reviews (auth required, validation, duplicate block), articleship profile GET/PUT, leave add/list with day math, practical-log CRUD + weekly + practical-to-syllabus correlations, firm-match ranking with big4 top matches, 9 community categories (6 level+3 cross), thread creation with level segmentation enforcement (Intermediate→final blocked with 403 as spec'd, Intermediate CAN post in intermediate/general/careers), verify-CA MOCK accepts 6-digit + bypasses final block, non-6-digit rejected 400/422, voting up/idempotent/flip/clear all correct upvote deltas, thread detail returns user_vote_thread+user_votes_reply, reply increments reply_count, study-groups list/create/join/leave with initials + is_verified_ca + is_member flags, weak-topic auto-schedule >=1 for P4/I5 papers (after wiping stale progress rows), Phase6RateLimitMiddleware enforces 5-reviews/24h with 429+Retry-After header, all Phase 1-5 regression endpoints intact (openapi, syllabus, radar/alerts, content/posts public; auth/me includes is_verified_ca+ca_membership_number).

minor.issue: auto_scheduled_flashcards returns 0 for I3 mocks with the demo user because the SEED already populates user_flashcard_progress rows for all 34 I3 cards — 22 are already-due-today and 12 are mastered (repetitions>=4), leaving 0 cards eligible to bring-forward per the auto-schedule algorithm. Spec review_request expected 'typically 10-15'. Weak_topics IS populated correctly (>=29 topics). Same feature works fine for P4/I5/F2/etc (returns 14 cards). Two possible fixes: (a) skew seed_flashcards.py so demo's I3 progress has a mix of future-due-not-mastered cards, OR (b) also count 'already due today' cards in auto_scheduled counter (semantically: they're still surfacing to the weak-topic priority queue).

action_items: OPTIONAL: Fix the I3 seed-exhaustion issue so `mock_i3_tax_*` submits produce auto_scheduled_flashcards >= 1 (see backend_issues.minor above). This is a seed-vs-algorithm mismatch, not a Phase 6 code defect — feature works on all other papers.

OPTIONAL: The review_request references data-testids `firm-card-violet-ca-journey`, `firm-view-violet-ca-journey`, `community-cat-violet-ca-journey`, `group-violet-ca-journey`, `match-firm-violet-ca-journey`, `dashboard-phase6-firms` (last one exists) — the `violet-ca-journey` suffix appears to be a stray copy-paste of the app subdomain rather than a real firm/category/group slug. Actual firms use slugs like `deloitte-india`, `gupta-gupta-ca`. No code fix needed — just noting the spec has some placeholder confusion.
critical_code_review_comments: routes_articleship.py & routes_community.py: Clean 417 + 399 lines respectively — good split from server.py. Pydantic body models with str_strip_whitespace + Field(min/max) validation are consistent.

server.py Phase6RateLimitMiddleware (lines 3279-3315): In-memory defaultdict(deque) buckets — will not survive backend restart and are not shared across workers. Fine for single-process dev but not production-safe. Recommend moving to Redis or slowapi's storage backend once you have multi-worker uvicorn.

routes_articleship.py::firm_match line 380: `_user = await require_user(request)` implicitly gates the endpoint behind auth despite the comment saying 'anyone can query'. If public read is intended, drop the require_user; if not, remove the misleading comment.

server.py mock_submit line 1105-1157: Weak-topic auto-schedule algorithm has an unstated tie-breaker gap — see backend_issues.minor. Consider adding a 'refresh existing due-today cards' path or a fallback (mark up to 15 not-yet-mastered regardless of current due state).
server.py line 3033: `is_verified_ca` reset to False on every /api/seed call — this is INTENTIONAL and useful for test resets (documented in test_credentials.md). Good.
routes_community.py verify_ca returns note='MOCKED: any 6-digit number is accepted in Phase 6.' — clear mocked-flag surfacing for the frontend badge. Good.
server.py: still 3487 lines — recommend continuing to split mocks/flashcards/content/dashboard into routers next iteration.

success_rate.backend: 97.7% (43/44 pytest passing, 1 skipped by design as rate-limit-consumed guard)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all Phase 6 pages load with correct testids, sidebar nav intact, dashboard bento present, editorial dark aesthetic preserved)

test_credentials: demo@cagrid.in / demo123 (Intermediate journey_level; POST /api/seed resets is_verified_ca=false).
seed_data_creation: Test-created ephemeral data: TEST study groups (~4-8, leftover in DB from creation tests but harmless), TEST forum threads in general/intermediate/careers/final categories, TEST leave records + practical logs on demo user, firm_reviews on ~6 random firms (wiped by TestPhase6RateLimit before its run). Consider adding cleanup fixture in future or a POST /api/seed enhancement to wipe TEST_-prefixed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_phase6.py is the canonical Phase 6 regression suite. Run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_phase6.py -n 0` (serial). Two tests intentionally restart backend to clear the in-memory Phase6RateLimitMiddleware bucket (TestPhase6RateLimit) — ensure sudo access. TestWeakTopicAutoSchedule uses mongo-direct progress wipe to avoid state-dependency on repeated runs. `sess_reset` fixture reseeds via /api/seed but does NOT invalidate sessions (verified in server.py::run_seed — no db.user_sessions.delete_many call). If /api/auth/login gets 429, wait 60s+ (5/min IP rate limit). All 41 firms + 9 forum categories + 5 seeded study groups verified. Verify-CA is MOCKED — any 6-digit number bypasses ICAI check.
rca of the issue: Weak-topic auto-schedule = 0 for I3 mocks. RCA: seed_flashcards.py pre-populates user_flashcard_progress for demo user on ALL 34 I3 cards. Distribution: 22 due-today (skipped by 'if due <= today: continue' branch), 12 mastered (skipped by 'repetitions>=4' branch). 0 cards remain eligible for bring-forward. Feature works correctly on other papers (P4=14, I5=14, F2=15, etc). Test proves this — test_submit_i3_flags_seed_exhaustion passes with auto_scheduled=0 and weak_topics=29. Mitigation options: (1) seed rebalance — reduce due-today progress rows and add some future-due unmastered rows for I3, (2) counter semantic fix — increment auto_scheduled for already-due cards too (they're validly re-surfaced), (3) reset progress in the seed's every-invocation branch. Option (1) is cleanest.
